prompt: I need to set up GitHub Actions to run terraform plan on PRs and terraform apply when they merge to main. We use GCP and our Terraform is in the infrastructure/ directory. Make sure it's secure.
assertions: Plan workflow triggers on pull_request with paths filter for infrastructure/
Apply workflow triggers on push to main with paths filter for infrastructure/
Authentication uses google-github-actions/auth with workload_identity_provider (not service account key)
id-token: write permission is present
All actions are pinned by commit SHA, not by tag
Apply workflow concurrency has cancel-in-progress: false
All terraform commands include -input=false flag
PR comment uses env variable for plan output, not direct interpolation in run block
permissions block is present and restrictive (contents: read at minimum)

prompt: We have a monorepo with Terragrunt. Our environments are in infrastructure/terragrunt/us-central1/dev/ and infrastructure/terragrunt/us-central1/prod/. I want dev to auto-apply on merge but prod to require manual approval. Set up the GitHub Actions workflows.
assertions: Workflow handles both dev and prod environments
Dev environment applies automatically (no manual gate)
Prod environment uses GitHub Environment protection with required reviewers or explicit approval step
Prod job depends on dev job (needs: or sequential)
Terragrunt is installed (setup action or manual)
Each environment has a separate concurrency group
Caching is configured for terraform plugins and/or terragrunt cache

prompt: Add drift detection for our Terraform infrastructure. It should run on a schedule, check if real infrastructure has drifted from the code, and open a GitHub issue if drift is found.
assertions: Workflow uses schedule trigger with cron expression
Uses terraform plan -detailed-exitcode to detect drift
Checks exit code 2 specifically (not just non-zero)
Creates a GitHub issue when drift is detected
Issue body includes plan output
Authentication uses OIDC/WIF (not static credentials)
issues: write permission is present
